mentions.note: Apple's AI deal with Alphabet makes Google the default search engine and extends Gemini integration. Neutral to positive for Alphabet as a beneficiary of AI deals.

Equity issuance to fund unprofitable data-center buildout raises dilution fears. Though $920M/month SpaceX deal shows Google Cloud customers prefer Nvidia GPUs over internal TPUs—a bullish signal for Nvidia offset by equity-supply concerns.
Just completed multi-billion fund raise for AI. Supply wave will impact stock in near term as investors raise cash.
Successfully raised $45B secondary offering with Goldman Sachs underwriting; huge demand, few flippers; stock bought back $45B last year, now selling $15B—capital deployment shift.
Successfully raised $45B of $85B sought; Berkshire's $10B participation validates story. Stock briefly rallied post-pricing; smart to front-load capital raise before SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI deals.
Shock $80B capital raise signals data center buildout severely underestimated costs; vulnerability in AI capex arms race.

Multiple revenue streams: YouTube (largest video company), Waymo (number-one self-driving), Google/Gemini search, Google Cloud (best horse to bet on); higher-priced multiple justified by diversification.
AI leader with hardware integration potential; part of sooner-to-be-public AI titan cohort alongside Anthropic/OpenAI; thriving in new semis-dominant era.
FANG stock and Magnificent Seven member; dominates search and digital advertising; rate-proof and recession-resistant.
High-quality growth name (formerly Google) that performed well during recession; example of company that works in thick or thin economic cycles.
AI models layer company (Gemini). Massive infrastructure spending on AI buildout, competing with other model developers for GPU capacity.

Ensured years of colossal profits from data center capex. Anthropic $200B chip/cloud deal sends stock soaring. Google Cloud critical winner; Gemini AI expanding to Apple ecosystem (160M US iPhone users).
